steps.title: Identify the structures
steps.content: The left side contains the subject “The powerful blast” (noun phrase). The right side begins with a prepositional phrase “from the collapse of the star's core under the force of its own gravity” and continues with “ejected particles,” where “ejected” is a verb with its object.

steps.title: Apply the correct form
steps.content: The blank must allow “ejected” to function as the main verb. A finite verb (results, resulted, had resulted) would create an independent clause — “The powerful blast resulted from the collapse...” — leaving “ejected particles” unattached to the sentence structure. A participle instead creates a modifying phrase: “The powerful blast resulting from the collapse...” that allows “ejected” to be the main verb. The finite verbs break the sentence into disconnected parts; the participle form enables a grammatical structure. That matches choice B — B is correct.
